channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Live: Dockers and Hawks battle for top-two spot in Perth blockbuster
item.description: The Dockers and Hawks meet in Perth in a blockbuster start to round nine in the AFL with both sides looking to consolidate a spot in the top two. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Chris De Silva

media:group.media:description: Shai Bolton of the Dockers in action during the round 18 AFL match between the Fremantle Dockers and Hawthorn Hawks at Perth Stadium on July 12, 2025 in Perth, Australia.

item.title: Concerns for competency of woman rescued in outback then arrested
item.description: Concerns have been raised about the competency of an elderly Indigenous woman facing a charge of unlawful wounding from 2024, after she was rescued and then arrested by police in Western Australia's outback this week.
item.dc:creator: Jarrod Lucas and Katrina Tap

media:group.media:description: Tjuntjuntjara is 700 kilometres north-east of Kalgoorlie.

item.title: Passengers who disembarked hantavirus cruise ship still unaccounted-for
item.description: A number of countries are scrambling to trace people who disembarked from an Atlantic cruise struck by a deadly virus outbreak before it was marooned off the coast of Cabo Verde, in an effort to prevent further spread of the disease.

media:group.media:description: MV Hondius, carrying a number of passengers suspected of having hantavirus infections, leaves Cabo Verde on Wednesday.

item.title: Two men guilty of Alice Springs teenager's New Year's Day murder
item.description: Two men have been found guilty of murder over the death of 19-year-old Yiel Deng Gatluak outside Alice Springs, while their teenage co-accused has been found guilty of the lesser offence of violent act causing death.
item.dc:creator: Chris Murrkarany Fitzpatrick

media:group.media:description: Two men have been found guilty of murdering 19-year-old Yiel Gatluak outside Alice Springs in 2024.

item.title: Ted Turner's infamous feud with Rupert Murdoch began in a Hobart boathouse
item.description: The two media moguls shared a bitter rivalry for decades after some drunken comments at a Hobart boathouse. Here's how their rivalry unfolded through the years.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Steedman

media:group.media:description: Ted Turner publicly feuded with Rupert Murdoch for decades.

item.title: Senior doctors write to Queensland's premier demanding cultural change
item.description: More than 100 senior clinicians from a major regional hospital raise concerns in a letter to the state government about hospital culture.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Dansie and Baz Ruddick

media:group.media:description: Townsville Hospital and Heath Service's CEO says a consulting firm had been engaged to improve the experience of staff.

item.title: Live: Illness forces late change and another spinal shake-up for Dogs
item.description: Matt Burton is ruled out of tonight's clash with the Dolphins with illness, meaning Sean O'Sullivan starts in the halves alongside Lachlan Galvin. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: Matt Burton is a late out.

item.title: 'We come together in unity': Nationwide vigils held for Kumanjayi Little Baby
item.description: Vigils have been held across the country in memory of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby, who was allegedly murdered in Alice Springs.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Ross, Jesse Thompson, Meg Whitfield, Monte Bovill, Tessa Mapstone, Elsie Lange, Sarah Maunder, and Pip Waller

media:group.media:description: Crowds gathered at vigils filled with pink and candles for the five-year-old girl.

item.title: Brisbane Lions player banned for four weeks for homophobic slur
item.description: Brisbane Lions player Koby Evans is banned for four games after being found guilty of conduct unbecoming for using a homophobic slur against an opponent in the VFL.

media:group.media:description: Lions player Koby Evans has apologised after receiving a four-match ban for making a homophobic slur in the VFL.

item.title: NSW woman seeks damages after alleging doctors amputated healthy leg
item.description: The woman says her leg was amputated despite it being "salvageable", but doctors argue the amputation was necessary to save her life.
item.dc:creator: Giselle Wakatama

media:group.media:description: Vesna Zvicer is seeking damages from Hunter New England Local Health District.

item.title: Pensioner accused of driving truck with 110 kilos of concealed cannabis
item.description: Police say the was pensioner driving a truck between Adelaide and Newcastle while carrying drugs with a street value of $720,000.
item.dc:creator: Wade Stephens

media:group.media:description: Police allege the 74-year-old man was transporting 110 kilograms of cannabis.

item.title: Breaking: Three ISIS-linked women taken to police stations after arrival in Australia
item.description: A group of Australian women and children with links to former ISIS fighters have begun arriving in Australia, nearly two weeks after leaving a refugee camp in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Bridget Rollason and Leonie Thorne

media:group.media:description: The Qatar Airways flight believed to be carrying a woman linked to ISIS fighters and her child lands in Sydney.

item.title: Clare Glade-Wright declared elected to Tasmania's upper house
item.description: Clare Glade-Wright unseats fellow independent Dean Harriss in the southern Tasmanian upper house seat of Huon.
item.dc:creator: Loretta Lohberger

media:group.media:description: Clare Glade-Wright has been elected to the Legislative Council, Tasmania's upper house of parliament.

item.title: $5.9 million to combat illicit tobacco, vapes in the NT
item.description: The federal government has given $5.9 million to the Northern Territory to "shut down sleazy, ridiculous shops selling illegal tobacco".

media:group.media:description: Australia Border Force seized 873 vapes in Darwin in April.

item.title: A tussle in Farrer, PM tries ACDC diplomacy
item.description: The heat is on in Farrer ahead of Saturday's by-election while in Canberra the prime minister has gone above and beyond with a gift for Japan's leader.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould

media:group.media:description: Scuffle ensues between Liberal senator and One Nation volunteer in Farrer.

item.title: Adelaide Crows to feed off returning skipper's energy
item.description: Adelaide expects to feed off Jordan Dawson's energy when the club captain returns for a rare trip to the MCG to take on Richmond.

media:group.media:description: Adelaide is hoping to bounce off the energy provided by returning skipper Jordan Dawson.

item.title: Qld premier rules out cabinet reshuffle amid ministers' relationship saga
item.description: Premier David Crisafulli is standing by two of his ministers who have insisted they did not breach conflict of interest rules over their on-again off-again relationship.
item.dc:creator: Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: Premier David Crisafulli has been quizzed on whether he knew about the ministers' relationship while in opposition.

item.title: Bribery and corruption barbs thrown as Solomon Islands PM voted out of office
item.description: Solomon Islands is set to have a new leader next week, with Jeremiah Manele voted out as prime minister after a fiery debate in parliament.
item.dc:creator: Chrisnrita Aumanu-Leong, Nick Sas, and Stephen Dziedzic

media:group.media:description: Jeremiah Manele served as Solomon Islands prime minister for two years before today's events.

item.title: DFAT says it has not been talking with Syria about return of 'ISIS brides'
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade says it has not had any contact with Syrian officials about the return of a group of Australian women and children with links to the Islamic State terrorist group.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Dick

media:group.media:description: A group of ISIS-linked Australians boarded a connecting flight from Doha.

item.title: Electoral watchdog quietly reopens investigation into Minns allegations
item.description: The NSW election watchdog has quietly reopened an investigation into allegations that NSW Premier Chris Minns conspired to evade state donations laws.
item.dc:creator: Dylan Welch

media:group.media:description: A recent affidavit alleged Chris Minns had accrued thousands of dollars in unreceipted donations and wanted to get them into his campaign coffers anyway.

item.title: Triple fatality bus crash driver found not guilty of dangerous driving
item.description: A jury has found Peter Jason Cafe not guilty of dangerous driving in relation to a triple-fatal Bruce Highway crash.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Chomicki

media:group.media:description: Three women were killed and two men were critically injured in the 2024 Greyhound bus crash.

item.title: Budget shows WA still rolling in cash with another bumper surplus
item.description: The WA government delivers a big-spending budget, focused on housing, health and cost of living relief including a $100 fuel handout, as a $2.4 billion surplus is unveiled. Here's what you need to know.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Mayes

media:group.media:description: WA Premier Roger Cook and Treasurer Rita Saffioti have unveiled the 2026-27 state budget.

item.title: Inmate dies in hospital after stabbing at western NSW prison
item.description: An inmate is charged with murder after allegedly stabbing a fellow inmate, who died in hospital overnight.
item.dc:creator: Lily Plass

media:group.media:description: Wellington Correctional Facility is 45km outside of Dubbo.

item.title: Brush turkey used for 'target practice' survives six months with arrow in back
item.description: A maimed brush turkey that was shot with a bow and arrow on the NSW Central Coast is rescued after wandering in to a resident's kitchen.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Forster and Mary-Louise Vince

media:group.media:description: The injured brush turkey has alluded East Gosford locals for months after being shot with a bow and arrow last year.

item.title: Indecision, secrets, and 'inevitability': Behind the latest 'ISIS bride' return
item.description: Advocates and the former Home Affairs boss say the return of another wave of so-called 'ISIS brides' was "inevitable". But a hands-off approach means Labor lost control of the narrative.
item.dc:creator: Sara Tomevska

media:group.media:description: The women and children left the Al Roj camp in Syria late in April, with plans to travel to Australia.
